title: Smoky Roasted Ghee Pilaf
description: A spicy and dense middle-eastern dish featuring dry sherry, deglazed with ghee for a show-off touch. This paleo-friendly creation is perfect as an appetizer or side dish.

ingredients.name: Pinch of sesame seeds for garnish (optional)

instructions: Rinse quinoa under cold water and transfer to a medium saucepan. Add 1 cup of water, bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover, and simmer until liquid is absorbed, about 15 minutes.
Heat ghee in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add onion and garlic, sauté for 2-3 minutes or until translucent.
Stir in smoked paprika, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper. Cook for an additional minute.
Deglaze the skillet with dry sherry, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan. Allow to simmer until liquid is almost completely absorbed.
Add cooked quinoa to the skillet, mixing well with vegetables and ghee mixture. Stir in dried apricots and cherries. Continue cooking for 2-3 minutes more or until everything is evenly combined.
If desired, sprinkle sesame seeds on top before serving. Serve warm as a side dish or appetizer.
